facts: The universe isn't just empty space; it's a flexible fabric called spacetime, proposed by Einstein.
Massive cosmic events, like two black holes colliding, create powerful ripples in this spacetime fabric – Gravitational Waves!
First detected by LIGO in 2015, from two black holes 1.3 billion light-years away merging. During the merger, about 3 solar masses were converted into pure energy as gravitational waves.
idea.payoff: We literally *heard* the universe vibrate, confirming Einstein's century-old prediction and opening a brand new window to the cosmos!
idea.title: The Universe Just *RANG*! 🌌🎶

scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with subtle, expanding ripple animation, like a stone dropped in water.

scenes.text: The universe is a flexible fabric: SPACETIME (Einstein's idea)
scenes.visual: Grid-like representation of spacetime, with a celestial body subtly deforming it.

scenes.text: Massive cosmic events (e.g., colliding black holes) create ripples: GRAVITATIONAL WAVES!
scenes.visual: Two black holes spiraling inward, generating prominent, expanding waves across the spacetime grid.

scenes.text: First detected by LIGO in 2015! (From 2 black holes merging 1.3 billion light-years away)
scenes.visual: Stylized graphic of the LIGO interferometer, with faint wave patterns approaching its arms. Small graphic of merging black holes in background.

scenes.text: We literally *heard* the universe vibrate! Confirming Einstein & opening a new window to the cosmos!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, vibrant nebula field with subtle, radiating wave patterns spreading outwards, symbolizing cosmic vibrations.
